Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Kids Empire Cheltenham (Pa) Llc'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Defendant Samantha E. Beatty And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $1000 Against Samantha E. Beatty; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
Off Calendar Dec-18-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing re: PLAINTIFF KIDS EMPIRE CHELTENHAM (PA) LLC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff Kids Empire Cheltenham (Pa) Llc'S First Set Of Form Interrogatories To Defendant Samantha E. Beatty And For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $1000 Against Samantha E. Beatty.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Plaintiff's motions to compel further responses to form interrogatory 15.1 is GRANTED. Defendants Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall serve on plaintiff's counsel full and complete supplemental responses to form interrogatory 15.1 by January 6, 2024. Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall collectively pay $1000 to Plaintiff by January 6, 2024, for the three motions to compel because they did not act with substantial justification.

"Each answer in the response must be 'as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits. If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible.'" Weil, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Pro. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2024 8:1047 (italics in original, quoting Code Civ. Proc. 2030.220(a)).

Here, "discovery has just begun" is not a proper response to the interrogatory. Defendants are obliged to state the facts that support their denials and defenses that they are presently aware of. If defendants presently do not have any facts, then they should say so. Defendants can reasonably ascertain the material allegations. (See Burke v. Superior Ct. of Sacramento Cnty. (1969) 71 Cal.2d 276, 281 ["a defendant in California courts may be required through discovery to disclose not only the evidentiary facts underlying his affirmative defenses . . . and denials . . . but also whether or not he makes a particular contention, either as to the facts or as to the possible issues in the case."].)

Order Signed/Filed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)

Plaintiff's motions to compel further responses to form interrogatory 15.1 is GRANTED. Defendants Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall serve on plaintiff's counsel full and complete supplemental responses to form interrogatory 15.1 by January 6, 2024. Reed Smith, LLP, Joseph Marger, and Samantha Beatty shall collectively pay $1000 to Plaintiff by January 6, 2024, for the three motions to compel because they did not act with substantial justification.

"Each answer in the response must be 'as complete and straightforward as the information reasonably available to the responding party permits. If an interrogatory cannot be answered completely, it shall be answered to the extent possible.'" Weil, et al., Cal. Practice Guide: Civ. Pro. Before Trial (The Rutter Group 2024) 8:1047 (italics in original, quoting Code Civ. Proc. 2030.220(a)).

Here, "discovery has just begun" is not a proper response to the interrogatory. Defendants are obliged to state the facts that support their denials and defenses that they are presently aware of. If defendants presently do not have any facts, then they should say so. Defendants can reasonably ascertain the material allegations. (See Burke v. Superior Ct. of Sacramento Cnty. (1969) 71 Cal.2d 276, 281 ["a defendant in California courts may be required through discovery to disclose not only the evidentiary facts underlying his affirmative defenses . . . and denials . . . but also whether or not he makes a particular contention, either as to the facts or as to the possible issues in the case."].)

The parties are advised if discovery disputes persist, the court, at the parties' expense, may appoint a discovery referee.

Order Signed/Filed.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (301/JMQ)
